name: Machine Learning Fundamentals II
description: Deepen your knowledge of data preprocessing, validation strategies, and advanced ensemble models.

questions.question: What is a primary consequence of leaving missing values untreated in datasets for most ML algorithms?
options: The model will automatically overfit the data.
The algorithm will fail or throw a runtime error.
The feature distribution shifts to a normal curve.
The training execution speed increases significantly.
questions.explain: Most standard machine learning libraries cannot handle missing inputs and will throw errors during training.

questions.question: Which imputation method fills missing numerical data with the most frequent value in that column?
options: Mean imputation
Median imputation
Mode imputation
Zero imputation
questions.explain: Mode imputation replaces missing data points with the most frequent value present in the feature.

questions.question: What is a major disadvantage of using One-Hot Encoding on high-cardinality categorical features?
options: It introduces severe data leakage issues.
It drastically increases feature dimensionality.
It forces the model to use linear structures.
It eliminates important target variable signals.
questions.explain: One-hot encoding creates a new binary column for every unique category, leading to a very sparse, high-dimensional matrix.

questions.question: When applying Target Encoding to a categorical variable, what risk must you actively mitigate?
options: Underfitting
Data leakage
Vanishing gradients
Sparsity errors
questions.explain: Target encoding uses information from the target variable to encode features, which can easily cause data leakage if not cross-validated.

questions.question: What is the primary utility of using a Pipeline in a machine learning workflow?
options: To automatically tune hyperparameters.
To enforce the exact same sequence of transformations.
To compress the final model file size.
To convert unsupervised tasks into supervised ones.
questions.explain: Pipelines bundle preprocessing and modeling steps together, ensuring identical data transformations during training and testing.

questions.question: How does using a Pipeline help prevent data leakage during cross-validation?
options: It fits transformers only on the training folds.
It removes all categorical features automatically.
It duplicates the validation target variables.
It normalizes the entire dataset beforehand.
questions.explain: Pipelines ensure preprocessing parameters are calculated strictly within each training fold, keeping validation folds isolated.

questions.question: What core machine learning technique does XGBoost utilize?
options: Deep stacking
Bagging ensembles
Gradient boosting
Feature scaling
questions.explain: XGBoost stands for Extreme Gradient Boosting, which sequentially trains trees to correct the errors of previous trees.

questions.question: Which strategy does XGBoost natively use to handle missing values during training?
options: It drops rows containing any missing values.
It learns the optimal default direction for them.
It replaces them with the mean of the column.
It stops execution and requests manual imputation.
questions.explain: XGBoost automatically learns whether missing values should go into the left or right child node based on training loss.

questions.question: Which of the following scenarios is a textbook example of data leakage?
options: Including target statistics from the test set in training.
Using a random seed to shuffle data before a split.
Training a model with highly correlated input features.
Removing outliers from the training set before fitting.
questions.explain: Data leakage occurs when information from outside the training dataset is inadvertently used to train the model.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of performing K-Fold Cross-Validation?
options: To compress the dataset for faster execution.
To get a stable estimate of model performance.
To completely eliminate the need for tuning parameters.
To generate synthetic features for small datasets.
questions.explain: K-Fold cross-validation reduces evaluation variance by averaging performance across multiple distinct data splits.
